intended_use: BriefCase is a radiological computer aided triage and notification software indicated for use in the analysis of chest CTs (with or without contrast) images in adults or transitional adolescents aged
intended_use_full: BriefCase is a radiological computer aided triage and notification software indicated for use in the analysis of chest CTs (with or without contrast) images in adults or transitional adolescents aged 18 and older. The device is intended to assist hospital networks and appropriately trained medical specialists in workflow triage by flagging and communicating suspect cases of three or more acute Rib fracture (RibFx) pathologies.
target_condition: Three or more acute rib fractures on chest CT
ground_truth: Ground truth determined by three senior board-certified radiologists using majority voting
other_metrics: Positive Likelihood Ratio (PLR): 14.2912 (95% CI: 8.614 - 23.710); Negative Likelihood Ratio (NLR): 0.0206 (95% CI: 0.005 - 0.082); Mean time-to-notification: 70.1 seconds (95% CI: 64.9-75.4); Additional Operating Point 1 - Sensitivity: 88.46% (95% CI: 80.71%, 93.89%), Specificity: 95.1% (95% CI: 91.17%, 97.62%); Additional Operating Point 2 - Sensitivity: 99.04% (95% CI: 94.76%, 99.98%), Specificity: 90.20% (95% CI: 85.27%, 93.91%)
